headline: Big consulting firms are getting real about AI deployment—this week showed consultants actually have to deliver, not just talk about it

stories.title: McKinsey makes candidates use AI Lilli in recruitment—now consultants gotta prove they can work WITH machines
stories.summary: McKinsey added an 'AI interview' stage where grads collaborate with Lilli during final rounds. They're literally testing if people can think alongside AI, not just write about it.
stories.takeaway: Competence with AI is officially table stakes at MBB—if you can't work with AI, you're not getting hired. McKinsey's 20,000 AI agents to 40,000 humans ratio shows this is fr the future of consulting.

stories.title: BCG drops AI logistics survey—shows how far adoption has come (and how far it still needs to go)
stories.summary: BCG published research on AI in logistics, revealing implementation progress from providers and shippers. Their survey lowkey shows the gap between talking about AI and actually using it in real operations.
stories.takeaway: Consulting firms are moving past hype into auditing actual deployment. When BCG publishes surveys, enterprises watch—this signals where consulting dollars are actually flowing.

stories.title: Accenture-Mistral deal expands 'sovereign AI' consulting play—giving Europe an alternative to US models
stories.summary: Accenture signed a multi-year deal with Mistral to co-develop enterprise AI solutions. They're training thousands of employees on Mistral's platform and selling it to clients who want data control.
stories.takeaway: Tbh this is bigger than a partnership—it's consultants betting that companies want AI they can control. With Accenture's global reach + Mistral's tech, they're positioning for the next consulting wave where compliance and sovereignty matter.

watchNext.title: OpenAI's Frontier Alliances with McKinsey, BCG, Accenture, Capgemini—consultants are now the distribution arm for enterprise AI
watchNext.why: This deal from February shows the real playbook: consultants help enterprises adopt OpenAI tech at scale. With Frontier AI agents launching, watch how these partnerships reshape consulting delivery models this quarter.

watchNext.title: Big consulting firms spending $10B+ on AI but still operating the old pyramid—the real transformation hasn't happened yet
watchNext.why: This analysis from Future of Consulting lays out the contradiction: firms spent billions but haven't restructured how they bill or staff. Understanding this gap is lowkey the best framework for seeing what actually changes next.
